name: openssh-client-ssh1
title: openssh-client-ssh1
summary: secure shell (SSH) client for legacy SSH1 protocol
description:
secure shell (SSH) client for legacy SSH1 protocol
This is the portable version of OpenSSH, a free implementation of
the Secure Shell protocol as specified by the IETF secsh working
group.
.
Ssh (Secure Shell) is a program for logging into a remote machine
and for executing commands on a remote machine.
It provides secure encrypted communications between two untrusted
hosts over an insecure network. X11 connections and arbitrary TCP/IP
ports can also be forwarded over the secure channel.
It can be used to provide applications with a secure communication
channel.
.
This package provides the ssh1 and scp1 clients and the ssh-keygen1
utility, all built with support for the legacy SSH1 protocol. This
protocol is obsolete and should not normally be used, but in some cases
there may be no alternative way to connect to outdated servers.
.
In some countries it may be illegal to use any encryption at all
without a special permit.
.
ssh replaces the insecure rsh, rcp and rlogin programs, which are
obsolete for most purposes.

arguments.description: Selects batch mode (prevents asking for passwords or passphrases).
arguments.raw: -B  Selects batch mode (prevents asking for passwords or passphrases).

arguments.description: Compression enable. Passes the -C flag to ssh(1) to enable compression.
arguments.raw: -C  Compression enable. Passes the -C flag to ssh(1) to enable compression.

arguments.description: Preserves modification times, access times, and modes from the original file.
arguments.raw: -p  Preserves modification times, access times, and modes from the original file.

arguments.description: mode: disables the progress meter as well as warning and diagnostic messages
arguments.raw: -q  mode: disables the progress meter as well as warning and diagnostic messages

arguments.description: Recursively copy entire directories. Note that scp follows symbolic links encountered
arguments.raw: -r  Recursively copy entire directories. Note that scp follows symbolic links encountered

arguments.description: Verbose mode. Causes scp and ssh(1) to print debugging messages about their progress.
arguments.raw: -v  Verbose mode. Causes scp and ssh(1) to print debugging messages about their progress.

commands.name: ssh-keygen1
commands.description: authentication key generation, management and conversion

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use scp1 because secure shell (SSH) client for legacy SSH1 protocol.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use scp1 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair scp1 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `scp1 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
